run.true_intent: I run customer support out of a Gmail inbox. I want it to read incoming support emails, summarise each one, label the urgent ones, and draft a reply for the top few — but I always want to review before anything is sent. Nothing auto-sends. I don't want it touching my personal email, just the support address.
run.vague_intent: make me a bot that helps with my emails

transcript.question: What should this bot actually do with your emails? Select all that apply.
options: Summarize / digest what's in my inbox
Draft replies for me to review
Remind me about emails I haven't responded to
transcript.answer: Triage and flag important emails, summarize them, and draft replies for me to review — all three of those. The reminders one I don't need.

transcript.question: Which email provider(s) should the bot work with?
If the bot drafts a reply, should it always wait for your approval before sending — or are there cases where it can send automatically?
options: Always require my approval before sending
It can send automatically for certain low-stakes replies
It should never send — drafts only
transcript.answer: Always require my approval before sending — nothing goes out until I've looked at it.

transcript.question: What makes an email 'important' enough to surface to you? Describe in your own words (e.g. from specific people, contains action items, mentions deadlines, etc.)
transcript.answer: I'd say an urgent one is anything where the customer sounds really frustrated or angry, mentions they can't use the product at all, or brings up refunds or cancelling. Also anything from a customer who's emailed more than once about the same thing.

capabilities.title: Email Triage & Flagging
tool_hints: gmail.messages.list — fetch unread messages from inbox since last triage watermark
gmail.messages.get — retrieve full message headers and body for each candidate
gmail.threads.get — pull full thread to detect repeat-contact patterns within the same topic
personas_database.query — look up prior contact records for a sender to check repeat-contact threshold
personas_database.upsert — write or update sender contact record (email, topic fingerprint, contact count, last seen) after each triage run

subscriptions.description: Fires when a new unread message lands in the inbox; used to trigger real-time triage in addition to scheduled polling.

trigger.description: Poll Gmail inbox every 5 minutes for unread messages not yet triaged; also supports manual invocation to re-triage on demand.

capabilities.title: Urgent Email Digest
tool_hints: get_message — fetch full body and headers for each flagged message ID so summaries are grounded in real content, never fabricated
read_rows — fetch triage records written by uc_triage, filtered to status IN ('urgent','follow_up') and not yet surfaced in a prior digest

subscriptions.purpose: Receive the list of newly flagged email IDs to summarize in this digest pass

trigger.notes: Digest is produced after uc_triage finishes a classification pass; also available on explicit user request

secondary.description: User may also request a digest at any time by asking for a summary of flagged emails

subscriptions.description: Primary input: the triage decision and the minimal email metadata needed to compose without a separate Gmail fetch when the snippet is sufficient.
User asks the bot to revise the draft with a note — triggers a rewrite and re-queues for approval without touching the original email.

trigger.description: Fires once per email thread each time uc_triage emits an urgent flag. Also accepts a manual trigger carrying a thread_id so the user can request a draft for any thread on demand.

run.true_intent: Exactly what I said: a daily 9am digest of the top 5 Hacker News stories, delivered to the in-app titlebar. Nothing more — no external publishing, no review step needed, it's just an informational read.
run.vague_intent: Every day at 9am, fetch the top 5 Hacker News stories and show them to me as a digest in the app titlebar. No need to publish anywhere else.

trigger.description: Every day at 9:00 AM local time

run.true_intent: Honestly I just have one concrete pain: every morning I copy yesterday's Stripe payouts into a Google Sheet by hand. I want that one thing automated — pull yesterday's Stripe payouts and append them to my 'Revenue' sheet each morning. Nothing else.
run.vague_intent: manage my whole workflow and make my life easier

transcript.question: Your intent is broad — let's start with one concrete job. Which would you want this persona to tackle first?
options: Morning briefing: surface what needs my attention across tasks, PRs, and calendar
Inbox triage: classify and prioritize emails so I see only what matters
Task prioritizer: pull open items from all my tools and rank them by urgency
PR watchlist: alert me when reviews are stalled or need my action
transcript.answer: Morning briefing sounds closest, but honestly what I really need is simpler — every morning I want yesterday's Stripe payouts pulled and added to my "Revenue" Google Sheet automatically. That's the one job.

transcript.question: You have both Linear and ClickUp connected. Which do you actually use for tracking your work tasks?
For calendar and meetings, which do you rely on?
transcript.answer: Google Calendar, but honestly that's not really what this is about — my thing is just about Stripe payouts and a spreadsheet, no meetings involved.

transcript.question: Should this persona ever take actions on your behalf automatically (e.g. reply to an email, post a Discord message, update a task status) — or always prepare a draft for you to approve first?
options: Always prepare a draft — I'll confirm before anything is sent or changed
Routine low-risk actions (status updates, task moves) can run automatically; messages need approval
Full autonomy — act without asking unless it's irreversible
transcript.answer: Always prepare a draft — I'll confirm before anything is sent or changed. Honestly it's just adding rows to my Google Sheet, so nothing risky, but I'd like to check it's doing it right first.

trigger.description: Runs every morning at 07:00 to fetch yesterday's payouts before the workday starts

run.true_intent: Exactly that: a weekday 9am standup that lists yesterday's merged PRs from our main repo and posts the summary to our #standup Slack channel. The only thing I didn't say is WHICH repo and WHICH Slack channel.
run.vague_intent: Every weekday at 9am, collect yesterday's merged pull requests from our repo and post a standup summary to Slack.

transcript.question: Which GitHub repository should be monitored for merged pull requests? Please provide the owner/repo (e.g. acme-corp/backend).
You don't have a Slack connector in your vault — where should the standup summary be posted?
Which specific Slack channel (or destination) should receive the daily summary?
transcript.answer: #standup — that's where the team already hangs out for morning check-ins.

transcript.question: Should the summary post to Slack automatically each morning, or should it be queued for your review before sending?
transcript.answer: Queue for my review first — at least for the first week or two while I make sure the summaries look right, then we can switch it to automatic.
